title: [Feature]: Evaluate headers-only delivery mode for metadata workflows
problem: Some workflows may need metadata-only processing, but nats-sinks is designed to persist message payloads and metadata safely. Headers-only delivery could reduce payload exposure for specific use cases, but it requires clear behavior for payload normalization, idempotency, DLQ content, and sink schemas.
proposal: Evaluate headers-only delivery support as an explicit mode for metadata-only workflows. The design should state whether sinks receive empty payloads, placeholder envelopes, or a distinct mode flag, and how DLQ and idempotency behave when payload bytes are intentionally absent.
users: Operators who need metadata-only custody workflows, maintainers evaluating NATS compatibility, and security reviewers interested in payload minimization.
delivery_semantics: ACK still must occur only after the metadata workflow has durably succeeded. Empty or absent payloads must be handled without crashes and without implying that the original payload was stored.
security: The feature may reduce payload exposure but metadata can still be sensitive. Documentation must avoid claiming full confidentiality and must explain what remains visible.
acceptance: A design decision documents headers-only behavior.
If implemented, configuration is explicit and validated.
Tests cover empty, absent, and metadata-only payload paths.
tests: Add envelope, runner, sink contract, and DLQ tests if implemented. Run scripts/check-sinks.sh and scripts/check.sh.
documentation: Update docs/architecture.md, docs/configuration.md, docs/security.md, docs/sink-framework.md, docs/nats-feature-gap-analysis.md, ROADMAP.md, and CHANGELOG.md.
closeout: Close after release or documented decision. Include release tag or decision, tests, documentation updates, and limitations.
